free_text: Neutron analysing powers and a low-energy optical
Polarization of neutrons emitted at 49dgrs taken
Proton recoil spectra were collected with

24 NE213 detectors, 5 cm in diam. and 15 cm long,
at angles 20-167 dgrs with an interval of 7 dgrs
Two flux monitors were used to normalize polarimeter
counting rates. A collimated beam monitor looking at
neutrons emitted at 49 degrees and a target yield
monitor viewing neutrons emitted at 90 degrees.
All of the optical model analysis has been made with
compound elastic scattering accounted for using a
recent formulation of the statistical model.
